metadata.notes: Standalone space-themed Clank. Modular board (6 modules, 3 mandatory + 3 variable double-sided), 3 factions (Outlaw, Resistance, Science), data cube hacking, Lord Eradikus rage track, market items, contraband.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Board & Modules
- **6 modular board pieces** — 3 mandatory (Cargo Bay, Command Module, Corridor) + 3 variable double-sided modules
- Board assembles differently each game for unique layouts

#### Cards
- **4 starting decks** of 10 cards each (6 Hack, 1 Access, 2 Stumble, 1 Scramble)
- **100 adventure cards** — the main deck for market row purchases and enemies
- **Reserve cards** — 3 always-available piles: Boldly Go, Memory Core, Fazerblade

#### Tokens & Cubes
- **120 Clank cubes** — 30 per player in 4 colors
- **24 black cubes** — neutral cubes for the boss bag
- **Boss bag** — black cloth bag for Clank resolution
- **8 data cubes** — 2 per player in matching colors
- **6 artifact tokens** — worth 5 to 30 VP
- **11 major secrets** and **28 minor secrets** — face-down bonus tokens
- **Command code tokens** — earned by hacking both data ports
- **Bounty hunter cubes** — placed on rage track
- **Blockade token** — blocks hyperlift access when Eradikus reaches final rage

#### Market Board
- **4 market item types** — Master Key, TelePass, Med Kit, Contraband (7 credits each)
- 2 of each for 3-4 players, 1 of each for 2 players

#### Player Components
- **1 pawn** per player
- **Health track** on player board — 10 health points
- **Boss marker** — tracks Lord Eradikus's rage level

subtopics.title: Game Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Overview
Clank! In! Space! is a deck-building adventure where 2-4 players sneak aboard Lord Eradikus's spaceship to steal valuable artifacts. You build your deck by purchasing cards, navigate a modular board, hack data ports, and race to escape before Lord Eradikus catches you.

#### Core Loop
Each turn you play 5 cards from your hand, generating four resources: **Skill** (buy cards), **Swords** (fight enemies), **Boots** (move through the ship), and **Clank** (noise that endangers you). After spending resources, discard and draw 5 new cards.

#### Win Condition
Steal an artifact from the Command Module, hack both data ports to earn a command code, and escape via an escape pod in the Cargo Bay. The player with the most victory points from artifacts, secrets, credits, cards, and the 20-point escape bonus wins.

#### Key Differences from Original Clank!
- **Modular board** — ship layout changes every game
- **3 factions** — Outlaw, Resistance, Science with combo card effects
- **Data cube hacking** — place your cubes on data ports for bonuses and command codes
- **Market items** — Master Key, TelePass, Med Kit, Contraband purchased with credits
- **Hyperlifts and Telepads** — additional movement options with restrictions
- **Bounty hunters and blockade** — escalation on the rage track

subtopics.title: Detailed Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Structure
1. **Play cards** — Play all 5 cards from your hand, generating Skill, Swords, Boots, and Clank
2. **Add Clank** — Place Clank cubes from personal supply to Clank area on board
3. **Spend resources** — Use Boots to move, Swords to fight, Skill to buy cards
4. **Collect tokens** — Pick up artifacts, secrets, or credits from your space
5. **Refill market** — Replace bought/defeated cards; boss attack symbols trigger attacks
6. **Discard and draw** — Discard hand, draw 5 new cards

#### Movement
- Each Boot moves 1 connection between spaces
- **Footprint icons** — require 2 Boots to cross
- **Sword paths** — require spending a Sword to pass through
- **Locked passages** — require a Master Key (market item, 7 credits)
- **Security checkpoints** — force you to stop movement
- **Hyperlifts** — connect central ship spaces; using one causes dizziness (no more Boots that turn)
- **Telepads** — require TelePass token (market item); using one causes dizziness

#### Buying Cards
- Spend Skill equal to card cost to add it to your discard pile
- Buy from the 6-card market row or 3 reserve piles (Boldly Go, Memory Core, Fazerblade)
- Multiple purchases allowed per turn
- Reserve piles never run out

#### Combat
- **Market enemies** (red banner) — spend Swords equal to strength, gain reward, enemy leaves game
- Enemies do NOT enter your deck

#### Hacking Data Ports
- 2 data ports exist in different ship modules
- When on a data port space, place 1 of your 2 data cubes on it
- Receive the bonus or penalty shown on the space
- After placing both data cubes, receive a **command code token**
- Command code is required to enter the Command Module (where artifacts are)
- Hacking advances the Boss Marker on the rage track

#### Faction System
- 3 factions: **Outlaw**, **Resistance**, **Science**
- Cards may have faction icons
- Playing a card with a faction icon activates faction bonus text on other cards of that faction played the same turn
- Example: Monkey Bot 30000 normally adds 3 Clank, but with a Science faction combo gets -3 Clank instead

#### Market Items (7 credits each)
- **Master Key** — opens locked passages permanently
- **TelePass** — allows use of telepads
- **Med Kit** — immediately heal 2 damage
- **Contraband** — worth VP at end of game
- May buy multiple items per turn (but not more than 1 of the same item)

#### Secrets
- **Major secrets** — spaceship plans (7 VP), sixth crystal (3 VP per power crystal), sonic grenades (opponents add 3 Clank each), health boosts, credit chips, brilliance flashes
- **Minor secrets** — speed boosts, combat boosts, multipass (activates all faction text), vaporizer (trash a card), archives (3 VP but advance boss marker)
- Picking up a secret does not end your movement

subtopics.title: End of Game & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Clank & Boss Attacks
- Cards with Clank icons add your colored cubes to the Clank area
- **Boss attacks trigger** when adventure row refill reveals a boss attack symbol
- All Clank area cubes dump into the boss bag
- Draw cubes equal to current rage track position — your color = 1 damage
- Black cubes are safe and returned to the bag

#### Rage Track Escalation
- Boss Marker advances when players hack data ports and steal artifacts
- When Boss Marker reaches **bounty hunter cubes**, they are dispatched to the Clank area as red cubes that deal damage to ALL players each attack
- When Boss Marker reaches the **final red zone**, the blockade token blocks hyperlift access to the Cargo Bay

#### Escape
- Requirements: carrying an artifact + command code token + reach escape pod in Cargo Bay
- Escaping earns **20 bonus VP**
- First escape or knockout triggers accelerated attacks — 4 cubes pulled per eliminated/escaped player each turn

#### Knocked Out
- 10 total damage = knocked out
- **In the Cargo Bay with an artifact** — rescued by allies, score points (no 20 VP escape bonus)
- **Deep in the ship or without artifact** — score **0 points**, everything lost

#### Final Scoring (escaped or rescued only)
- Artifact VP (5-30)
- Secret token VP
- Credits (1 VP each)
- Card VP from purchased cards in your deck
- Escape bonus (20 VP if escaped via pod)
- **Tiebreaker:** most valuable artifact

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Route Planning
Before your first move, scan the board for three things: where the 2 data ports are, where the artifacts are in the Command Module, and where the escape pods are in the Cargo Bay. Plan a rough loop that hits both data ports on the way to the artifacts and passes near an escape pod on the way back. Teleport pads and hyperlifts are shortcuts — factor them into your route.

#### When to Turn Back
This is the most important decision in the game. A good rule: grab your artifact when you still have at least 5 health remaining. Below 5 health, head for an escape pod immediately. A 20-point artifact you escape with is worth more than a 30-point artifact that gets you knocked out (which scores 0). Watch other players — if someone is close to escaping, the countdown is about to start.

#### Card Priorities
- **Boldly Go** (reserve) — Boots + Skill in one card, excellent early buy
- **Boot cards** — movement is king; you need Boots to reach data ports, artifacts, and escape
- **Trash effects** — removing Stumble cards improves every future draw
- **Faction combos** — cards like Monkey Bot 30000 flip from liability to asset with the right faction pairing
- **Avoid** buying expensive cards late that won't cycle into your hand before the game ends

#### Clank Management
Minimize Clank in the early game when the bag has few cubes and yours will be drawn often. Your 2 Stumble cards are pure liability — trash them whenever possible. In the late game near an escape pod, generating Clank matters less since you're about to leave. Time your noisiest turns for right before you escape.

#### Hacking Strategy
Hack data ports on the way in, not on the way back. Backtracking to hack ports wastes precious turns while boss attacks keep coming. Each hack gives you a bonus or penalty and advances the Boss Marker, so be aware of the rage track position before you hack.

#### Market Items
Master Key opens locked shortcuts. TelePass enables telepads for fast movement. Med Kit heals 2 damage, potentially saving you from knockout. Contraband is pure VP. Prioritize Master Key and TelePass early if your planned route needs them; Med Kit is great insurance mid-game.

#### Common Mistakes
- Diving deep without hacking data ports first (forces backtracking)
- Ignoring the Boss Marker position on the rage track
- Buying cards you don't need just because you have Skill
- Forgetting that first escape triggers accelerated attacks for everyone
- Not buying movement cards early enough

rulings.ruling: Picking up a secret token does not end your movement for the turn. If you still have Boots or teleport ability, you may keep moving after collecting a secret.
Using either a Telepad or a Hyperlift causes dizziness — you cannot use any Boots for the remainder of that turn after teleporting or using a hyperlift.
Buying from the Market is an action. You may buy more than one item per turn if you have the credits, but not more than one of the same item type in a single turn.
You must hack both data ports and receive a command code token before you can enter the Command Module where artifacts are located. Without a command code, you cannot access artifacts.

Playing a card with a faction icon (Outlaw, Resistance, Science) activates the faction bonus text on other cards of that same faction played during the same turn. Each faction bonus activates at most once per turn.

If knocked out in the Cargo Bay with an artifact, you are rescued and still score your points (but no 20 VP escape bonus). If knocked out anywhere else or without an artifact, you score 0 points for the entire game.

Bounty hunter cubes sit on the rage track. When the Boss Marker reaches them, they are dispatched to the Clank area as red cubes. Once in the bag, red bounty hunter cubes deal 1 damage to ALL players (not just one) each time they are drawn.

When the Boss Marker reaches the final red zone on the rage track, the blockade token is placed on the hyperlift connecting to the Cargo Bay, blocking that route. Players must find alternate paths to escape.
A player who reaches an escape pod in the Cargo Bay while carrying an artifact and holding a command code earns 20 bonus victory points in addition to all other scoring.

Each player receives 2 data cubes. You place one data cube per data port space when you are on that space. You receive the bonus or penalty shown immediately. After placing both cubes, you receive a command code token.
